panels.description: Wide shot: A massive Martian tripod stands motionless against a dark, starry sky, looking down upon the Earth with a cold, detached mechanical gaze. The atmosphere is icy and intellectual, with blue and silver highlights on the metal surfaces.

bubbles.text: But the Martians were lifted above all these organic fluctuations of mood and emotion.
Their preference for men as nourishment is explained by the remains of the victims they brought with them from Mars.

panels.description: Medium shot: A clinical, top-down view of a Martian victim's remains—a bipedal creature with a fragile, sponge-like white skeleton, a large round head, and sunken flinty eye sockets. It lies broken within a dark metallic cylinder fragment.

bubbles.text: These creatures were bipeds with flimsy, silicious skeletons and feeble musculature, standing about six feet high.
On our planet, the mere attempt to stand would have broken every bone in their bodies.

panels.description: Medium shot: The silhouette of the Narrator sitting at a Victorian desk, illuminated by a single candle. He is writing in a journal; behind him, a wall is covered in anatomical sketches of alien biology.

bubbles.text: I may add certain further details to enable the reader to form a clearer picture of these offensive creatures.
In three other points their physiology differed strangely from ours.

panels.description: Wide shot: Martians working tirelessly inside a glowing pit at night, their tentacles manipulating complex machinery. In the extreme foreground, a macro view of black ants crawling over a clod of dirt, mirroring the tireless alien labor.

bubbles.text: Their organisms did not sleep. They had little or no sense of fatigue.
In twenty-four hours they did twenty-four hours of work, as is the case with the ants.

panels.description: Close up: A detailed view of a Martian's torso where a smaller, bulbous version of itself is physically budding out from the skin, like a lily bulb or a plant. Wet, biological textures with soft, eerie backlighting.

bubbles.text: Wonderful is the existence of the sexless.
A young Martian was found attached to its mother, partially budded, just as a young lily bulb is budded.

panels.description: Wide shot: A split-screen composition. On the left, a soft-focused image of a human family silhouette. On the right, the harsh, red-tinted landscape of Mars with its budding, strange inhabitants. The contrast emphasizes evolutionary divergence.

bubbles.text: In man, this method of reproduction has been superseded by the simpler method.
On Mars, however, just the reverse has apparently been the case.
